behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is entirely within a single function that parses a template string and checks for forbidden patterns. No module boundary is crossed. REAL-INFRA: No OS, network, or subprocess. MOCK-HIDE: Nothing to mock — the input is a string or file path, the output is pass/fail. LLM-DEP: No model call. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered but rejected because the lint check operates on a single artifact (the template text) with no cross-module state handoff required.
behaviors.test_description: Load a representative prompt template containing a verbatim SKILL.md phase instruction. Invoke the lint function directly. Assert it returns a rejection/error indicating phase-instruction leakage. Also run the linter against a clean template and assert it passes. No harness, no LLM, no filesystem beyond reading a fixture string.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure only appears after the harness's interpolation pipeline and fixture loader have both run and composed their outputs. A unit test on either module alone would not catch leakage introduced at the seam. REAL-INFRA: The harness reads skill/fixture files from disk, but an in-process temp-file approach is sufficient — no real network or subprocess. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking the interpolation step would defeat the purpose; real assembly is required. LLM-DEP: No model call needed — the check is on assembled text, not on model output. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered but rejected because no real subprocess or network is required; in-process assembly with temp fixture files is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real harness interpolation pipeline and fixture loader together using temp fixture files. Invoke the full prompt-assembly path. Capture the resulting prompt string and run the same phase-instruction lint check from B1 against it. Assert no leakage is present. Do not call any model.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure — hard-coded rules appearing in the test invocation instead of file-loaded context — only manifests when the plugin/skill file loader and the test scaffolding are wired together. Unit testing either in isolation does not catch the wrong source being used. REAL-INFRA: Skill files on a real (or temp) filesystem are needed, but in-process temp files are sufficient. LLM-DEP: The behavior is about structural source-of-truth for the test, not about what the model outputs; a stub suffices to confirm the invocation path reads from files. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic was considered because 'validates behavior from that context' could imply model execution, but the behavior describes the invocation structure, not model output quality — that question is addressed in B5 and B8.
behaviors.test_description: Set up a temp skill-file tree and a stub model endpoint. Invoke the test harness with a user-style prompt and capture what context object reaches the model stub. Assert the context originated from the temp skill file, not from any literal string in the test source. Assert no phase instruction from SKILL.md appears verbatim in the assembled test prompt.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is at the module boundary between the harness's skill-variant loader and its prompt-execution path — specifically that the test prompt text stays constant while the skill file changes. A unit test cannot confirm the harness successfully routes the same prompt through different skill-file configurations. REAL-INFRA: Requires writing two skill-file variants (full and degraded/absent) to a temp directory and running the harness against both. No real network or subprocess. LLM-DEP: A stub model endpoint is sufficient; the assertion is about execution success and prompt-text invariance, not about model output quality. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered but rejected because no real subprocess or OS primitive is needed — temp files and in-process harness invocation cover the failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Create two temp skill-file variants: one complete and one with the relevant section removed. Point the harness at each in turn, supply the same user-style prompt both times, and stub the model endpoint. Assert both runs complete without error and that the prompt string sent to the stub is identical in both runs. Assert the only thing that differs between runs is the skill context injected into the harness state.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The behavior's own failure mode is that a real model no longer exhibits the expected behavior when skill context is removed. MOCK-HIDE: A stub always returns a fixed response regardless of skill context, so a stub would always pass — the very regression being guarded against is invisible without the real model. LLM-DEP: The regression only appears because the real model's behavior degrades when skill guidance is absent. A deterministic fixture replaces the model's actual judgment with a constant, making the test permanently green even when the skill has genuinely regressed. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered but rejected because 'the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt' is a direct statement that correctness depends on what the real model produces; no concrete behavior text disqualifies the Agentic level.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full behavioral test suite against a real model endpoint twice: once with the canonical SKILL.md and once with the relevant section removed or replaced with a degraded placeholder. Assert that the suite passes on the canonical skill and fails on the degraded variant under the identical user-style prompt. Do not alter the test prompt between runs.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure (raw output not captured, or captured after parsing) only appears when the capture step and the parsing pipeline are wired together in the correct order. A unit test on the capture function alone cannot confirm it runs before the parser. REAL-INFRA: Writing to a temp file or in-memory artifact is sufficient; no real OS event or subprocess needed. LLM-DEP: A stub returning a known raw string is sufficient to verify capture ordering — correctness does not depend on model output quality. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered because 'artifact' could imply a real file on disk, but in-process temp files and an in-memory capture buffer reproduce the exact failure mode (capture-before-parse ordering) without needing real OS primitives.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real capture module and JSON-parsing pipeline together with a stub model client that returns a known raw string. Instrument the pipeline to record execution order. Assert the artifact (temp file or in-memory buffer) contains the raw string before the parser is invoked. Assert the artifact is non-empty even when the raw string is invalid JSON that causes the parser to raise an exception.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode is entirely within the assertion logic itself — whether it checks schema paths/field placement rather than just substring presence. No cross-module interaction is required to expose this. REAL-INFRA: None. LLM-DEP: Sample output fixtures (hand-crafted dicts or strings) are sufficient to prove structural assertions reject outputs that keyword checks would accept. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered because assertions eventually run inside a harness, but the behavior's own failure — assertion logic that only checks keywords — is fully exposed by invoking the assertion functions directly against crafted fixtures. Nothing in the behavior text requires multi-module wiring to catch this failure.
behaviors.test_description: Craft two model-output fixtures: one that contains the expected keyword but violates the schema structure (e.g., a role-specific field in the wrong schema path), and one that is structurally correct. Invoke the assertion functions directly against both. Assert the first fixture fails the structural assertions and assert the second passes. Verify that a keyword-only check would pass the first fixture, confirming structural assertions add genuine coverage.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This behavior is specifically about a discrepancy between keyword results and structural results on real model output. A stub always returns a constant — it cannot exhibit the property of satisfying keywords while violating structural constraints in a realistic way. MOCK-HIDE: A deterministic fixture replaces actual model judgment with a constant, permanently hiding the regression the test is meant to catch. LLM-DEP: The entire point is that the real model can produce output that passes keyword checks but fails structural checks; this property requires real model execution. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered and rejected because no concrete text in the behavior disqualifies Agentic — the phrase 'under live model execution' directly states the dependency on real model output, and 'catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss' states the failure is only visible with real model output.
behaviors.test_description: Run the behavioral test suite against a real model endpoint with both a keyword-only assertion variant and a structural assertion variant active. Induce a known structural regression (e.g., by degrading the skill section governing field placement). Assert the structural assertions fail while the keyword-only assertions pass, confirming structural checks add genuine signal over keyword checks under real model execution.
